summary.title: Car Buying Patterns
summary.description: The average selling price of cars has increased over the years, with a mean of 4.26. The number of unique car makes and models has also increased, with an average of 13.67 and 285.11 per year, respectively. The correlation between year and selling price is 0.39, indicating a positive relationship.

insights.title: Increasing Average Selling Price
insights.description: The average selling price of cars has increased over the years, with a mean of 4.26. This trend is expected to continue, with a correlation between year and selling price of 0.39.

insights.title: Decreasing Correlation Between Year and Km Driven
insights.description: The correlation between year and km driven is -0.31, indicating a negative relationship. This could be a risk for car manufacturers and sellers, as it may indicate a decrease in car usage over time.

insights.title: Shift in Car Buying Patterns Towards Diesel Cars
insights.description: The average number of diesel cars per year is 443.77, which is higher than the average number of petrol cars per year, which is 358.11. This indicates a shift in car buying patterns towards diesel cars.

insights.title: Positive Correlation Between Engine and Max Power
insights.description: The correlation between engine and max power is 0.79, indicating a strong positive relationship. This suggests that cars with more powerful engines tend to have higher max power.
